executiveSummary: Enhancement to Stock Buddy MCP server enabling MCP clients to provide their own market data instead of relying on server-side data fetching. This decoupled architecture allows clients to use any data source while leveraging Stock Buddy's powerful analysis capabilities.
problemStatement: Current Stock Buddy implementation requires the MCP server to fetch data from predefined sources, limiting flexibility for clients who have access to premium data feeds, proprietary databases, or specific broker APIs. Clients need the ability to provide their own real-time or historical data while still utilizing Stock Buddy's comprehensive analysis tools.
targetUsers: MCP client developers with custom data sources
Trading platforms integrating Stock Buddy analysis
Institutional traders with proprietary data feeds
Quantitative analysts with custom data pipelines
Mobile app developers needing lightweight integration
objectives: Enable MCP clients to provide market data directly in tool calls
Maintain backward compatibility with existing data providers
Implement robust data validation for client-provided data
Create clear data contracts for each analysis tool
Ensure security by removing server-side credential storage
inScope: Enhanced MCP tool schemas with data requirements
Data validation layer for client inputs
outOfScope: Data encryption in transit (handled by MCP)
constraints: Must validate all client data before processing
Performance must handle datasets up to 5 years of daily OHLCV
Error messages must be clear and actionable

requirements.title: Client Data Provider Interface
requirements.description: Create a ClientDataProvider that accepts market data directly from MCP tool arguments, bypassing server-side data fetching while maintaining the standard DataProvider interface for compatibility

requirements.title: Enhanced Tool Input Schemas
requirements.description: Update all MCP tool schemas to explicitly define required data fields (ohlcv, fundamentals, news, etc.) with detailed type information and validation rules
requirements.rationale: Clients need clear contracts for data provision

requirements.title: Data Validation Layer
requirements.description: Implement comprehensive validation for client-provided data including type checking, range validation, date consistency, and required field verification
requirements.rationale: Ensures analysis quality regardless of data source

requirements.title: Backward Compatibility
requirements.description: Maintain support for existing file-based and database providers while adding client data capability through provider factory pattern

requirements.title: Client Implementation Examples
requirements.description: Provide example client code in Python, JavaScript, and TypeScript showing how to fetch data from various sources and call Stock Buddy tools

requirements.title: Performance Optimization
requirements.description: Optimize data handling to efficiently process large datasets (5+ years of daily OHLCV) without memory issues or timeouts

requirements.title: Error Handling and Messaging
requirements.description: Implement detailed error messages for data validation failures, missing required fields, and malformed data with clear remediation guidance
requirements.rationale: Clients need clear feedback on data issues

requirements.title: Data Contract Documentation
requirements.description: Create comprehensive documentation of data contracts for each tool including field names, types, formats, optional/required status, and example payloads

requirements.title: Security Considerations
requirements.description: Ensure client-provided data is sanitized, prevent injection attacks, validate data sizes to prevent DoS, and remove any server-side credential storage
requirements.rationale: Client data could be malicious or malformed

requirements.title: Migration Guide
requirements.description: Provide step-by-step migration guide for existing users to transition from server-fetched to client-provided data including configuration changes and code examples

glossary.definition: A data provider implementation that receives market data from MCP client tool calls rather than fetching from external sources
Formal specification of required and optional data fields for each analysis tool
A provider that passes client-provided data directly to analysis skills without modification
JSON Schema definition of input parameters and data requirements for an MCP tool

changes: Initial PRD for client-provided data enhancement
